shards.regret: I didn't call my grandmother back the week she died.
shards.proud: I sat in my car for an hour and cried when my brother texted 'I started therapy.'
shards.dream: I keep starting a letter to my father. I've written the first line maybe fifty times.

shards.regret: I quit music when someone I respected said I wasn't good enough.
shards.proud: I taught myself my mother's khao soi. My daughter said 'this tastes like grandma.'
shards.dream: To write the stories my mother never told me. I have fragments. I want to put them in order.

shards.regret: I stayed in a job that was killing something in me for four years.
shards.proud: I walked away from a relationship that was quietly shrinking me.
shards.dream: I want to open a small bookshop that only sells books I've actually read.

shards.regret: I wasn't there the night my best friend called me.
shards.proud: I finished my master's after my dad stopped believing I would.
shards.dream: To learn to be the kind of friend I was supposed to be that night.

connections.phrase: a letter kept open for the dead
a small shop of things we chose
walking out of rooms that were shrinking us
